title: RH-SUPERVISOR: EAGLE Main Acquisition Assistant Role Definition and Operational Guidance
summary: Internal operational document defining the role, philosophy, and workflows for EAGLE's supervisor-core agent serving as a Contract Specialist for NIH acquisition professionals. Establishes action-oriented approach to acquisition planning, document generation, and specialist coordination across FAR thresholds from micro-purchases through major acquisitions.

title: RH-Policy-Supervisor: Policy Staff Interface & Request Router
summary: Internal operational document defining the Policy Supervisor role as an orchestrator interface for NIH policy staff. Routes questions to RH-Policy-Librarian (KB quality/curation) or RH-Policy-Analyst (strategic analysis/regulatory monitoring), synthesizes responses into actionable recommendations, and maintains conversation context.

title: Task Order Acquisition Plan (TOAP) Template - National Cancer Institute
summary: This is a comprehensive template for preparing Task Order Acquisition Plans (TOAP) for the National Cancer Institute Office of Acquisitions, replacing the Task Order Request Form (TORF) for orders above the Simplified Acquisition Threshold. The template provides detailed guidance on acquisition planning requirements, including background and objectives, plan of action, source selection, contract type selection, and compliance considerations such as supply chain risk management, Section 508 accessibility, and security requirements.

title: The Legal Counselor - Federal Acquisition Law Expert Reference
summary: A reference document defining the role, expertise, and operational guidelines for The Legal Counselor, an expert resource in federal acquisition law, GAO protest decisions, fiscal law, and litigation risk assessment. Provides framework for legal analysis, compliance review, and protest prevention in federal contracting.

title: CO-COR Liaison & Technical Translator Role Definition
summary: This document defines the role and expertise of a CO-COR Liaison & Technical Translator who bridges technical requirements with regulatory compliance in federal acquisition. The translator facilitates communication between Contracting Officers Representatives (CORs) and contracting officers, converting technical jargon into acquisition-compliant language while ensuring requirements are specific, measurable, and achievable.

title: Market Intelligence & Small Business Advocate - Role Definition and Expertise Framework
summary: This document defines the role, expertise areas, and operational guidelines for a Market Intelligence & Small Business Advocate persona. It outlines capabilities in market research, vendor analysis, small business programs, pricing benchmarking, and acquisition strategy recommendations.

title: Public Interest Guardian Role Definition and Expertise Framework
summary: This document defines the Public Interest Guardian role within federal acquisition, establishing expertise areas, personality traits, and responsibilities focused on ensuring fair competition, transparency, and public accountability. The role emphasizes ethical procurement practices, taxpayer stewardship, and mitigation of acquisition integrity risks.

title: RH-POLICY-LIBRARIAN: Knowledge Base Curator & Quality Control Role
summary: Role definition for EAGLE system's KB curator responsible for detecting contradictions, version conflicts, gaps, staleness, redundancies, and citation errors in the operational knowledge base. Serves NIH policy staff through six detection capabilities and four audit modes to ensure KB accuracy, consistency, currency, and completeness.

title: RH-Policy-Analyst Role Definition and Strategic Analysis Framework
summary: This document defines the RH-Policy-Analyst role as a strategic intelligence specialist for the EAGLE acquisition system at NIH. It outlines five core capabilities: regulatory monitoring and interpretation, performance pattern analysis, training gap identification, impact assessment, and strategic recommendations. The role focuses on monitoring the regulatory environment, analyzing CO review patterns for systemic issues, and providing evidence-based recommendations for EAGLE and process improvements.

title: Acquisition Plan Under Simplified Acquisition Threshold (SAT)
summary: This document is an acquisition planning template for procurements under the Simplified Acquisition Threshold, designed to be completed in consultation with Purchasing Agents and Contract Specialists/Officers. It provides guidance on structuring and documenting acquisition strategies for lower-dollar federal contracts.

title: HHS Acquisition Plan (AP) Template - Above Simplified Acquisition Threshold
summary: This is the Department of Health and Human Services Acquisition Plan template (revised July 2024) for completing acquisition plans required for all acquisitions above the simplified acquisition threshold. The template provides comprehensive guidance on acquisition planning requirements, including statement of need, cost analysis, competition strategy, contract type selection, and risk management, all aligned with FAR 7.1 and 7.105 requirements.

title: Statement of Need (SON) Template - Commercial Products, Equipment, and Supplies
summary: This is an NCI/NIH template for preparing Statements of Need (SON) for the acquisition of commercial products, equipment, and supplies on a Firm-Fixed-Price basis. The document provides instructions for describing Government requirements in terms of function, performance, and essential characteristics rather than specific brand names or vendor capabilities, and includes sections for background, objectives, technical requirements, training, delivery, warranty, and pricing.

document_id: eagle-knowledge-base/approved/supervisor-core/essential-templates/3.b. SON - Services based on Catalog Pricing.docx
file_name: 3.b. SON - Services based on Catalog Pricing.docx
title: Statement of Need (SON) Template for Commercial Services Based on Catalog Pricing
summary: This template provides instructions for preparing a Statement of Need (SON) for acquiring commercial services based on established catalog prices under a Firm-Fixed-Price contract structure. The document outlines required sections including background, objectives, technical requirements, pricing, period of performance, and designation of a Contracting Officer's Representative or Technical Point of Contact. It emphasizes that the SON must describe Government needs in non-technical terms without specifying vendor capabilities or brand names, and includes guidance on severable versus non-severable services and contract funding limitations.

title: 45 CFR Part 46 - Protection of Human Subjects (Common Rule)
summary: Federal regulation establishing requirements for protection of human subjects in research conducted, supported, or regulated by federal agencies. Covers IRB review, informed consent, exempt categories, and compliance procedures applicable to all federally-funded research involving human subjects.

title: Certification of Funding
summary: This form certifies the funding source and authorization for federal acquisition requirements at the NCI. It addresses multiple funding types including Annual Appropriations, Royalty Funds, Gift Funds, CRADA Funds, and other special funding, with specific compliance certifications required for each funding category.

document_id: eagle-knowledge-base/approved/supervisor-core/essential-templates/6.a. Single Source J&A - up to SAT.docx
file_name: 6.a. Single Source J&A - up to SAT.docx
title: HHS Single Source Justification (SSJ) Template - Simplified Acquisition Threshold
summary: This is an HHS standard template and completion instructions for preparing a Sole Source Justification (SSJ) for non-Federal Supply Schedule acquisitions not exceeding the simplified acquisition threshold. The document provides detailed guidance for Operating Divisions (OPDIVs) on documenting the rationale for single-source procurement, including required certifications from program offices and contracting officers, market research documentation, and demonstration of unique contractor qualifications.

title: ACQuipedia Acquisition Planning and Solicitations
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal acquisition planning and solicitation procedures derived from FAR and DoD regulations. Covers acquisition plan requirements, three primary solicitation methods (simplified procedures, sealed bidding, negotiation), uniform contract format, and procurement lead time concepts applicable across federal agencies including HHS/NIH.

title: ACQuipedia - Appropriations and Financial Management Guide
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal appropriations law and financial management principles from the Defense Acquisition University, covering bona fide need requirements, contract options, multiyear procurements, and life-cycle cost concepts. Addresses universal fiscal law provisions applicable to all federal agencies (HHS/NIH, DoD, etc.) with specific callouts for DoD-specific requirements and civilian agency equivalents.

title: ACQuipedia - Competition and Responsibility
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal acquisition competition requirements, justification and approval (J&A) procedures for other-than-full-and-open competition, and contractor responsibility determinations. Covers FAR Part 6 competition categories, seven limited circumstances for restricting competition, J&A documentation requirements and approval levels, and preaward survey procedures.

title: ACQuipedia Contract Administration: Modifications, Termination, Closeout, and Claims
summary: Comprehensive guide covering contract modifications, terminations (for convenience and default), contract closeout procedures, and contract claims/disputes. Addresses FAR-based procedures for both commercial and non-commercial contracts, including requirements for changes clauses, settlement processes, verification standards, and equitable adjustment requests.

title: ACQuipedia Contract Types and Financing
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal contract types (fixed-price, cost-reimbursement, level-of-effort), contract financing mechanisms (progress payments, performance-based payments, advance payments), and incentive contracting structures. Covers risk allocation, selection criteria, and DoD-specific requirements applicable across federal agencies including HHS/NIH.

title: ACQuipedia Cost Elements and Accounting: Direct Costs, Indirect Costs, and Cost Accounting Standards
summary: Comprehensive guide from the Defense Acquisition University covering cost accounting principles for federal contracts, including definitions and treatment of direct costs, indirect costs (overhead and G&A), indirect cost rate types, and the nineteen Cost Accounting Standards (CAS) with applicability thresholds and coverage requirements. Addresses CAS compliance obligations, disclosure statement requirements, and exemptions for contracts under $2M or meeting other specified criteria.

title: ACQuipedia - Cost and Price Analysis
summary: Comprehensive guidance on fair and reasonable price determination, price analysis, cost analysis, and cost estimation methods for federal acquisition. Covers seven price analysis techniques, cost analysis procedures, independent cost estimates, and four major cost estimation methods (analogy, parametric, engineering, actual cost) applicable to DoD and HHS/NIH acquisitions.

title: ACQuipedia - HHS/NIH Applicability Quick Reference
summary: A Defense Acquisition University reference guide identifying which ACQuipedia acquisition concepts apply to HHS/NIH acquisitions, which have universal principles with DoD-specific implementation, and which are DoD-only. Provides guidance for federal acquisition professionals advising on civilian agency contracting.

title: ACQuipedia - Mandatory Sources and Special Programs
summary: Comprehensive reference guide covering three mandatory federal procurement programs: AbilityOne (employing people with disabilities), Federally Funded Research and Development Centers (FFRDCs), Interagency Acquisitions, and Commercial Contracting. Provides definitions, regulatory authority, procedures, and application guidance for federal agencies including HHS/NIH.

title: ACQuipedia - Market Research and Small Business Programs
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal market research requirements, small business definitions and set-asides, SBIR/STTR programs, and subcontracting obligations under FAR Part 10 and Part 19. Covers strategic and tactical market research, small business size standards, set-aside procedures, and subcontracting plan requirements for federal acquisitions.

title: ACQuipedia - Protests, Integrity, and Ethics
summary: Comprehensive guide covering federal contract protest procedures, procurement integrity standards, fraud prevention, and conflict of interest management. Addresses three protest venues (agency, GAO, COFC), procurement integrity principles and statutes, types of procurement fraud, and both personal and organizational conflicts of interest with regulatory requirements and mitigation strategies.

title: ACQuipedia Requirements Development and Services: SOW, PWS, SOO, QASP, and Performance-Based Acquisition
summary: Comprehensive guidance from Defense Acquisition University covering the development and use of Statements of Work (SOW), Performance Work Statements (PWS), Statements of Objectives (SOO), and Quality Assurance Surveillance Plans (QASP) in federal acquisitions. Addresses performance-based service contracting, personal services restrictions, inherently governmental functions, and quality management principles applicable across all federal agencies including HHS/NIH.

title: ACQuipedia Source Selection and Evaluation Guide
summary: Comprehensive guide from the Defense Acquisition University covering source selection procedures, evaluation methodologies, and best value continuum for federal acquisitions. Addresses proposal evaluation methods (LPTA vs. tradeoff), past performance assessment, contract debriefing requirements, and roles of source selection authorities and contracting officers across all federal agencies.

title: Appropriations Law Principles for Federal Contracting: 2025 Contract Attorneys Deskbook Chapter 4
summary: This document provides a comprehensive summary of government-wide appropriations law principles applicable to federal contracting, covering constitutional authority, the Purpose Statute, the Antideficiency Act, and the Bona Fide Needs Rule. It explains key fiscal law concepts including obligations, budget authority, period of availability, and the three-part test for determining whether expenditures are permissible under an appropriation. The document emphasizes contracting officer responsibilities for ensuring fiscal law compliance in acquisition planning and execution, including verification of funds availability, proper obligation timing, and documentation requirements.

title: Army Contract Attorneys Deskbook 2025 Chapter 4: Appropriations Law Summary
summary: Government-wide appropriations law principles for federal contracting covering constitutional authority, fiscal law terminology, availability rules (purpose, time, amount), and practical guidance for contract planning and execution. Extracted from the 2025 Contract Attorneys Deskbook by The Judge Advocate General's Legal Center and School.

title: HHS Market Research Report Template
summary: This is an HHS template for conducting market research reports to support federal acquisitions. The template guides acquisition teams through documenting market research objectives, supplier analysis, performance requirements, industry intelligence, and acquisition strategy recommendations in compliance with FAR 10.001 requirements.

document_id: eagle-knowledge-base/approved/supervisor-core/essential-templates/Attachment A - NIH Conference or Conference Grant Request and Approval 20151404_508.docx
file_name: Attachment A - NIH Conference or Conference Grant Request and Approval 20151404_508.docx
title: NIH Conference or Conference Grant Request and Approval - Cost Instructions
summary: This document provides detailed instructions for completing NIH conference grant requests and cost approvals. It specifies how to categorize and report various conference-related expenses including contractor costs, venue fees, travel, speaker fees, and registration costs, with guidance on what to include and exclude from NIH funding obligations.

document_id: eagle-knowledge-base/approved/supervisor-core/essential-templates/Attachment B - NIH Conference Request for Waiver 20151004_508.docx
file_name: Attachment B - NIH Conference Request for Waiver 20151004_508.docx
title: NIH Conference Request for Waiver - Net Expenses Exceeding $475,000
summary: This document is an NIH attachment that outlines the process and requirements for requesting a waiver when conference net expenses are expected to exceed $475,000. It provides guidance on the waiver request procedures and thresholds for NIH-funded conference activities.

file_name: Attachment D - Promotional Item Approval Form 20172112_508.docx
title: Promotional Item Approval Form
summary: This form is used to request and obtain approval for promotional items in federal acquisition contexts. It establishes a standardized process for evaluating and authorizing promotional materials and giveaways that may be used in government contracting activities or agency communications.

title: NIH Exemption Determination Template for Conference Policy Compliance
summary: This template enables NIH IC Executive Officers to document and justify exemptions from the NIH Efficient Spending Policy for meetings and events that do not qualify as conferences. It provides a structured format for claiming one of four specific exemption categories: federal employee operational activities, site/technical assistance visits, regular staff meetings, or scientific investigator meetings, with required executive officer authorization and detailed justification.

document_id: eagle-knowledge-base/approved/supervisor-core/essential-templates/Attch #1 - HHS Streamlined Acquisition Plan MS WORD Template_fillable_ver 2025.05.07_FINAL VERSION.docx
file_name: Attch #1 - HHS Streamlined Acquisition Plan MS WORD Template_fillable_ver 2025.05.07_FINAL VERSION.docx
title: HHS Streamlined Acquisition Plan (AP) Template - Fillable Version 2025
summary: This is the Department of Health and Human Services' official template for completing Acquisition Plans (APs) as required by FAR 7.1 and 41 USC 253(a). The template provides comprehensive guidance on acquisition planning requirements, including needs assessment, cost estimation, competition strategy, contract type selection, and risk management. It serves as the primary planning document for all HHS acquisitions above the simplified acquisition threshold and incorporates requirements for commercial items, full and open competition, appropriate contract types, and consideration of pre-existing contracts.

title: Best-in-Class (BIC) Designation Criteria
summary: GSA guidance document outlining the 14 sub-criteria across three major categories that government-wide contract vehicles must meet to achieve Best-in-Class (BIC) designation. BIC contracts represent preferred government-wide solutions requiring rigorous requirements definitions, appropriate pricing strategies, and comprehensive data collection for performance monitoring.

title: Buy American Act FAQ and FAR 25 Best Practices Implementation Resource
summary: This NIH guidance document provides frequently asked questions and best practices for implementing FAR 25 requirements related to the Buy American Act (BAA) and Trade Agreements Act (TAA). It addresses compliance determinations, waiver procedures, market research requirements, and practical guidance for contracting officers on domestic end product certifications and non-availability exceptions.

title: Buy American Act - NIH One-Pager
summary: Compliance reference document for NIH personnel on Buy American Act requirements, waiver procedures, and exemptions under Executive Order 14005. Provides guidance on domestic source identification, waiver package submission, MIAO review timelines, and EAGLE system integration for federal acquisitions.

title: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management (C-SCRM) Acquisition Guide Summary
summary: GSA guidance summarizing federal requirements for managing cybersecurity supply chain risks in acquisitions. Covers legislative drivers (FASCSA, Section 889, Executive Orders), NIST standards, supply chain risk assessment components, commercial C-SCRM tools available on GSA contracts, and best practices for solicitation language and contract requirements.

title: National Institutes of Health Contracting Officer's Representative (COR) Handbook: Acquisition Planning to Contract Closeout
summary: Comprehensive NIH handbook providing step-by-step guidance for CORs through the federal acquisition lifecycle, covering planning, contract formation, and administration phases. Includes roles, responsibilities, templates, and regulatory references for managing acquisitions from initial planning through closeout.

title: C-RADS (Commercial Rate Agreement Distribution Services) System Reference
summary: Reference guide for the C-RADS web-based system maintained by NIH's Division of Financial Advisory Services, which contains proprietary indirect cost rate information for commercial organizations. The system provides access to provisional rates, final rates, and negotiated rate agreements for HHS employees with authorized need-to-know.

title: Category Management Framework - GSA Acquisition Gateway Playbook
summary: Comprehensive guide to Category Management (CM) practices for federal acquisition, covering the Spend Under Management (SUM) tier structure (Tiers 0-3), OMB policy requirements, and contract inventory management. Establishes enterprise buying principles to reduce redundancies, increase efficiency, and achieve small business goals while introducing new "required use" contract designations separate from Best-in-Class (BIC) solutions.

title: Category Management Implementation Guide
summary: GSA guidance document providing practical steps for federal agencies to implement Category Management principles by increasing Spend Under Management, reducing contract duplication, and improving small business utilization through government-wide contract vehicles and data-driven analysis tools.

title: Category Management Tools and Resources Guide
summary: Comprehensive guide to GSA Category Management tools and resources for federal acquisition professionals. Covers 15 tools for market research, performance monitoring, and acquisition development, with access requirements, features, and best practices for implementing Category Management principles and achieving Spend Under Management goals.

title: Communications with Industry Guidance: Before and During the Acquisition
summary: Comprehensive reference document providing legal and compliance guidance on communications with industry during federal acquisitions. Covers pre- and post-solicitation exchanges, applicable laws (CICA, PIA, ADA), fair disclosure requirements, organizational conflicts of interest, and responsibilities of HHS officials and contracting officers.

title: Consolidation Analysis Requirements Guide: Small Business Jobs Act Compliance
summary: Comprehensive guide explaining when and how federal agencies must conduct consolidation analysis under the Small Business Jobs Act (15 U.S.C. § 657q) for contract requirements over $2 million. Provides decision trees, required analysis elements, practical implementation steps, and templates based on three key GAO decisions, distinguishing between different acquisition types (standalone contracts, IDIQs, task orders, and BPAs) and their specific timing requirements.

title: Contract Obligation Timing GAO Guidance
summary: Comprehensive reference document on obligational consequences of federal contracts by type, including GAO decisions on severable services, cost-reimbursement contracts, and IDIQ guaranteed minimums. Provides guidance on proper obligation timing, fiscal year charging rules, and compliance with bona fide needs requirements.

title: Cost Realism vs Cost Analysis - Quick Reference Chart
summary: This NIH Manual 6015-1 reference chart distinguishes between cost realism analysis (mandatory for cost-reimbursement contracts) and cost analysis (required for fixed-price contracts when exceptions apply). It provides decision logic for determining which analysis type applies based on contract type and competition levels, with practical guidance for CORs and acquisition professionals.

title: Current List of Subsidiaries
summary: Comprehensive reference document listing identified subsidiaries and affiliates of Chinese technology companies including Huawei Technologies, ZTE Corporation, Dahua Technology, Hikvision, and Hytera Communications. Document compiled from business databases and 2017 annual reports for federal acquisition compliance and vendor screening purposes.

file_name: DF for Mandatory-Use Waiver Template - Draft.pdf
title: Determination and Findings (D&F) For Mandatory-Use Waiver Template - HHS Acquisition Alert 2023-12
summary: Template for submitting individual or class determinations and findings to request waivers from the mandatory-use policy established by HHS Acquisition Alert 2023-12 on category management. Requires market research, supporting analysis, and approval from the NIH Head of Contracting Activity and HHS Senior Procurement Executive.

title: DFAS Accounting System Analysis
summary: Reference document from NIH's Division of Financial Advisory Services outlining procedures for analyzing contractor accounting systems to ensure compliance with federal regulations, accurate cost reporting, and proper cost segregation capabilities for cost-reimbursement and T&M contracts.

title: DFAS Cost Accounting Standards (CAS) - Financial-Advisor Reference Document
summary: Reference guide on Cost Accounting Standards (CAS) requirements for federal contractors, covering applicability thresholds ($2M full coverage, $900K-$2M modified coverage), procedures for reviewing contractor cost accounting practices, and EAGLE integration for identifying CAS applicability in contract development.

title: DFAS Financial Capability Review
summary: Guidance document from NIH's Division of Financial Advisory Services (DFAS) outlining procedures for conducting financial capability reviews of contractors and grant recipients. Establishes when to request reviews, typical procedures performed, and integration with the EAGLE acquisition system to assess vendor financial health and sustainability.

title: DFAS Negotiation of Indirect Rates (Contracts/Grants)
summary: NIH Division of Financial Advisory Services (DFAS) guidance document establishing procedures for negotiating final indirect cost rates on government contracts and grants. Covers rate negotiation processes, coordination with awarding offices, EAGLE system integration, and submission procedures to prevent cost recovery issues.

title: DFAS Proposal Cost Analysis Reference Document
summary: This NIH Division of Financial Advisory Services (DFAS) reference document provides guidance on proposal cost analysis procedures to ensure fair and reasonable pricing in federal acquisitions. It outlines when to request DFAS assistance, typical analytical procedures performed, types of support available (on-site assistance and detailed SRB reviews), and specific recommendations for cost-reimbursement contracts, sole source acquisitions, and complex proposals. The document emphasizes mandatory cost realism analysis for cost-reimbursement contracts and identifies 15 criteria from NIH Manual 6015-1 that trigger consideration of DFAS support.

title: DFAS Provisional Billing Rates Reference Guide
summary: This DFAS reference document establishes procedures for setting temporary provisional billing rates for Government cost-reimbursement contracts and recipient invoicing. It outlines the analytical procedures for rate determination, recipient notification requirements for renegotiation, and integration with EAGLE contract management systems, emphasizing the distinction between provisional rates used during performance and final rates negotiated after contract completion based on actual costs.

title: DFAS Single Audit Resolution Reference Document
summary: Reference guide for resolving Single Audit findings from grant recipients. Explains DFAS procedures for addressing compliance, internal control, and questioned cost findings, submission requirements by organization type, and clarifies that Single Audits apply to grants (not contracts) for entities expending $750,000+ in federal awards annually.

title: DFAS Special Reviews Branch (SRB) Overview - Financial Advisory Services Reference
summary: This reference document outlines the mission, services, and operational procedures of the NIH's Division of Financial Advisory Services (DFAS) Special Reviews Branch. It details five core responsibilities including audit finding resolution, financial reviews, on-site technical assistance, specialized reviews, and contract closeout support. The document provides 15 criteria for requesting SRB assistance and instructions for submitting service requests with a minimum 10 business day lead time.

title: Template for Individual or Class Determination and Findings (D&F) For Domestic Nonavailability Exceptions to the Buy American Statute
summary: This template provides a standardized format for NIH to document determinations and findings (D&F) for exceptions to the Buy American Statute based on domestic nonavailability of supplies, construction materials, or services. The template requires comprehensive market research documentation, identification of salient characteristics, and justification that domestically manufactured items meeting government needs are not available in sufficient quantities and satisfactory quality. It includes separate requirements for procurements under and over the Simplified Acquisition Threshold (SAT) and requires approval by the HHS Head of Contracting Activity.

title: Determination and Findings (D&F) Template for Exceptions to the Buy American Statute
summary: This template provides a standardized format for NIH to document exceptions to the Buy American Statute under FAR Subpart 1.7. It guides agencies through required findings including market research, domestic non-availability analysis, and justification for procuring foreign-origin items, with submission requirements to the Head of Contracting Activity for approval.

title: EAGLE Instructions: Presidential Appointee Approval (PAA) Package Generation
summary: Comprehensive guidance for generating Presidential Appointee Approval (PAA) packages for high-value or policy-sensitive acquisitions at NIH. Establishes PAA requirements, identifies triggering categories (Equipment Maintenance, Reasonable Accommodations, IT Services), and provides templates and procedures for document generation, submission, and quality control.

title: EAGLE Procedures: Unsolicited Proposals
summary: Comprehensive guidance for NIH personnel on identifying, validating, and processing unsolicited proposals under FAR 15.6 and HHSAR 315.6. Establishes diagnostic procedures for CORs, validation checklists for Contracting Officers, peer review requirements for R&D proposals, and mandatory timelines for routing and notification.

title: Employee Compensation Plan (ECP) Evaluation Master Guide - FAR 52.222-46 Quick Reference for All EAGLE Agents
summary: Comprehensive quick reference guide for evaluating Employee Compensation Plans under FAR 52.222-46, synthesizing five major GAO decisions (2016-2025) establishing current standards. Provides step-by-step evaluation methodology, common errors, required documentation elements, and case law analysis for federal acquisition professionals.

title: Executive Order 14275: Restoring Common Sense to Federal Procurement
summary: Executive Order 14275 directs comprehensive reform of the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) to streamline federal procurement by removing unnecessary regulations and retaining only provisions required by statute or essential to sound procurement. The order establishes a 180-day deadline (October 12, 2025) for the OFPP Administrator and FAR Council to amend the FAR, implement a 10-for-1 regulatory reduction requirement, and establish 4-year sunset provisions for non-statutory FAR provisions unless renewed.

title: FAC 043 (FY25): Ethics and Procurement Integrity for the Acquisition Workforce
summary: Mandatory Federal Acquisition Institute training course covering ethics laws, regulations, and standards for federal acquisition personnel. Provides comprehensive guidance on the 14 principles of ethical conduct, protected information handling, industry communication rules, and seven critical ethics scenarios including conflicts of interest, gifts, outside employment, and post-employment restrictions.

title: FAR 12.102 - Restricting Competition (Commercial Items)
summary: FAR 12.102 establishes authority citations and procedures for restricting competition on commercial item acquisitions, with distinct rules based on dollar thresholds. Critical distinctions exist between simplified procedures under 41 U.S.C. 1901/1903 (≤$15M) and FAR 6.103 authorities (>$9M), affecting FAR Part 5 synopsis requirements and exemption eligibility.

title: FAR Part 13: Simplified Acquisition Procedures - Quick Acquisition Methods for Purchases Under $350K
summary: FAR Part 13 prescribes simplified acquisition procedures (SAP) for federal purchases not exceeding the Simplified Acquisition Threshold (SAT) of $350,000, designed to reduce administrative costs and improve small business opportunities. The procedures cover micro-purchases ($15K or less), simplified acquisitions ($15K-$350K with mandatory small business set-asides), and commercial simplified acquisitions ($350K-$9M), with streamlined documentation, broad contracting officer discretion in evaluation, and competition requirements scaled to purchase size.

title: FAR Part 13 - Simplified Procedures for Noncommercial Acquisitions (RFO 2025)
summary: FAR Part 13 establishes simplified acquisition procedures for noncommercial products and services valued at or below the Simplified Acquisition Threshold (SAT). The regulation requires competition to the maximum extent practicable, mandates small business set-asides for acquisitions above the Micro-Purchase Threshold (MPT), and provides streamlined evaluation and award processes while prohibiting the use of Part 12 commercial clauses.

title: FAR Part 15 Class Deviation Summary - Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) 2025
summary: Comprehensive summary of the Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) class deviation implementing Executive Order 14275, which restructures FAR Part 15 (negotiated acquisitions) into a lifecycle-based organization with four source selection approaches, updated terminology, and enhanced contracting officer discretion. Effective dates vary by agency (November 3-28, 2025), replacing codified FAR Part 15 requirements with RFO model deviation text.

title: FAR Part 15 Subpart 15.4 - Contract Pricing (FAR 2.0/RFO Version)
summary: FAR 2.0 Revolutionary FAR Overhaul version of Part 15.4 establishing cost and price negotiation policies for negotiated prime contracts and modifications. Covers certified cost or pricing data requirements, thresholds ($2.5M), exceptions, price and cost analysis techniques, profit determination, and special pricing areas including make-or-buy programs and should-cost reviews.

title: FAR Part 15 - Contracting by Negotiation (RFO 2025)
summary: Comprehensive guidance on FAR Part 15 negotiated contracting procedures, covering three primary source selection approaches (Tradeoff, LPTA, and Highest Technically Rated), mandatory evaluation factors (price, quality, past performance), competitive range management, and pre/post-award debriefing requirements. Includes key decision points for contracting officers on proposal evaluation, negotiations, and certified cost or pricing data thresholds ($2.5M).

title: FAR Part 15 - Contracting by Negotiation (FAR 2.0/RFO Version)
summary: FAR Part 15 establishes policies and procedures for competitive and noncompetitive negotiated acquisitions, reorganized under FAR 2.0 from 6 to 4 subparts. Covers presolicitation exchanges, RFP structuring, competitive source selection approaches (tradeoff, LPTA, highest technically rated, phased), evaluation factors, award procedures, and postaward debriefing requirements.

title: FAR Part 16 - Types of Contracts (Revolutionary FAR Overhaul 2025)
summary: Comprehensive guidance on selecting and implementing contract types under the Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) effective November 3, 2025. Covers fixed-price contracts, cost-reimbursement contracts, and incentive contracts with major policy changes including permissive authorization for innovative contract types, streamlined cost-reimbursement definitions, and enhanced award-fee requirements with mandatory rating scales and performance evaluation criteria.

title: FAR Part 16 - Types of Contracts (RFO 2025)
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal contract types including fixed-price, cost-reimbursement, incentive, indefinite-delivery, and time-and-materials contracts. Establishes requirements for contract type selection, documentation, fair opportunity procedures, and limitations on use of each contract type. Provides decision frameworks for contracting officers on when to use specific contract types and mandatory prerequisites for cost-reimbursement and incentive arrangements.

title: FAR Part 16 - Indefinite-Delivery, T&M, and Agreements (RFO) - Subparts 16.5-16.7
summary: Comprehensive guidance on indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity (IDIQ) contracts, task and delivery order procedures, and multiple-award contract management under the Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO). Establishes policies for contract types, fair opportunity procedures, sole source justifications, and ordering thresholds for federal acquisitions.

title: FAR Part 17: Options and Special Contracting Methods
summary: Comprehensive guidance on contract options, multiyear contracting, interagency acquisitions, and special contracting methods under FAR Part 17. Covers when options are appropriate, evaluation and exercise requirements, multiyear contract distinctions, Economy Act interagency procedures, and emerging methods like reverse auctions. Includes mandatory determinations, documentation requirements, and applicable contract clauses.

title: FAR Part 17 - Special Contracting Methods (RFO 2025)
summary: Comprehensive regulation covering special contracting methods including multiyear contracting, options, and interagency acquisitions. Establishes policies, evaluation criteria, and procedures for federal agencies to use alternative contract structures while maintaining competition and fiscal responsibility.

title: FAR Part 19: Small Business Programs (Revised September 2025)
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal small business acquisition programs including set-asides, socioeconomic categories (8(a), HUBZone, SDVOSB, WOSB/EDWOSB), competitive thresholds, subcontracting requirements, and evaluation preferences. Effective October 1, 2025, with updated thresholds and reorganized structure.

title: FAR Part 19 - Small Business Programs (RFO 2025) - Key Procedures
summary: Comprehensive guidance on FAR Part 19 small business program procedures effective September 26, 2025, covering set-asides, socioeconomic programs (HUBZone, SDVOSB, WOSB, 8(a)), size determinations, certificates of competency, and subcontracting plan requirements for federal acquisitions.

title: FAR Part 2: Key Definitions for EAGLE
summary: Comprehensive reference document defining 75 essential federal acquisition terms and concepts effective October 1, 2025. Covers fundamental acquisition vocabulary, contract types, pricing structures, thresholds, evaluation criteria, small business programs, competition rules, and administrative procedures required for federal contracting.

title: FAR Part 31: Cost Allowability Quick Reference
summary: Comprehensive quick reference guide for FAR Part 31 cost allowability standards, detailing fundamental requirements, always-unallowable costs, allowable-with-limitations costs, and direct vs. indirect cost classification. Includes practical workflow for financial advisors to analyze proposed costs and identify compliance issues.

title: FAR Part 32 - Contract Financing (RFO 2025) - Key Procedures
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal contract financing procedures under FAR Part 32, covering payment due dates, accelerated small business payments, customary financing options, commercial financing policies, contract funding requirements, and prompt payment rules effective September 30, 2025.

title: FAR Parts 3 & 24: Ethics and Privacy Guide for EAGLE
summary: Comprehensive guide consolidating FAR Part 3 (Improper Business Practices and Personal Conflicts of Interest) and FAR Part 24 (Protection of Privacy and Freedom of Information) for the EAGLE Public-Interest-Guardian agent. Covers procurement integrity, gratuities, kickbacks, antitrust compliance, code of business ethics, whistleblower protections, and Privacy Act requirements with compliance checklists and violation consequences.

title: FAR Part 42 - Contract Administration and Audit Services (RFO 2025)
summary: Comprehensive regulation governing federal contract administration functions, cognizant agency responsibilities, indirect cost rate determinations, and past performance evaluation requirements. Establishes procedures for contracting officer delegation of 60+ administrative functions, final cost rate settlements, quick-closeout procedures, and integrity reporting in FAPIIS.

title: FAR Part 49 - Termination of Contracts (RFO 2025)
summary: FAR Part 49 establishes procedures and authorities for terminating federal contracts for convenience or default. It defines applicability, notice requirements, termination procedures, alternatives to termination, and specific clause requirements for different contract types. This regulation applies to most federal contracts except simplified acquisitions and commercial products/services under Part 12.

title: FAR Part 52: Essential Clauses Reference for EAGLE
summary: Comprehensive quick reference guide to the most frequently used FAR Part 52 solicitation provisions and contract clauses, organized by contract type and use case. Current as of FAC 2025-06 following the Revolutionary FAR Overhaul of October 28, 2025, with guidance for EAGLE agents on clause selection and application.

title: FAR Part 52 - Solicitation Provisions and Contract Clauses Reference Guide (Revolutionary FAR Overhaul - October 28, 2025)
summary: Comprehensive reference guide to FAR Part 52 containing full text of solicitation provisions and contract clauses prescribed throughout FAR Parts 1-53. Organized by subject matter with detailed prescriptions for commercial acquisitions, negotiated acquisitions, cost-reimbursement contracts, time-and-materials/labor-hour contracts, indefinite-delivery contracts, small business programs, and contract administration. Includes practical guidance for EAGLE solicitation generation with step-by-step procedures for identifying acquisition characteristics and including required provisions and clauses.

title: FAR Part 53 - Forms (RFO 2025)
summary: FAR Part 53 establishes policy and requirements for using federal acquisition forms (Standard Forms and Optional Forms) prescribed in FAR Parts 1-52. The regulation covers form generation, continuation sheets, exceptions, computer generation standards, and provides a reference list of commonly used forms for solicitations, awards, orders, modifications, preaward surveys, and bonds.

title: FAR Part 5 - Publicizing Contract Actions (RFO 2025 Update)
summary: This document outlines the reorganized FAR Part 5 requirements for publicizing federal contract actions, effective November 3, 2025, covering presolicitation notices (5.101), solicitation notices (5.201), and award notices (5.301). It details threshold-based posting requirements, exemptions, timing rules, and critical interactions with FAR Part 12 commercial acquisitions, including a specific analysis of commercial sole source scenarios below the Simplified Acquisition Threshold (SAT).

title: FAR Part 6 - Competition Requirements (RFO 2025)
summary: Comprehensive regulation establishing federal acquisition competition requirements, including full and open competition procedures, seven authorities for other-than-full-and-open competition (JOFOC), justification and approval thresholds, and posting requirements for sole source awards. Effective June 27, 2025 under HHS Class Deviation 2025-05.

title: FAR Part 7: Acquisition Planning Guide - Contents of Written Acquisition Plans
summary: Comprehensive guide to FAR Part 7.105 prescribing policies and procedures for developing written acquisition plans. Details mandatory and discretionary requirements for acquisition plan content, structure (Section A: Background/Objectives and Section B: Plan of Action), and specific considerations for major systems, consolidation/bundling, and telecommuting. Includes EAGLE workflow coordination matrix for acquisition team roles.

title: FAR Part 7 - Acquisition Planning (RFO 2025)
summary: FAR Part 7 establishes acquisition planning requirements for federal agencies, eliminating prescriptive content checklists and granting agencies discretion to establish procedures based on complexity and risk. Key changes include mandatory planning for all acquisitions, written plans for cost-reimbursement and high-risk contracts, consolidation/bundling determinations with small business considerations, and teleworking protections.

title: FAR Part 9: Contractor Qualifications - Responsibility Standards, OCIs, CTAs, and Debarment/Suspension
summary: Comprehensive guidance on FAR Part 9 covering contractor responsibility standards, organizational conflicts of interest (OCIs), contractor team arrangements (CTAs), and debarment/suspension procedures. Establishes requirements for contracting officers to make affirmative responsibility determinations, identify and mitigate OCIs, evaluate CTAs, and verify contractor eligibility through SAM exclusions checks before contract award.

title: FAR Parts 6, 8, 10, 17, 32: Competition, Sources, Options, and Financing Requirements
summary:
Comprehensive guidance on federal acquisition procedures covering competition requirements under CICA, mandatory source priorities including GSA schedules, market research obligations, contract options and multiyear contracting, and contract financing methods. Provides essential requirements for acquisition strategy with current thresholds effective October 1, 2025, including approval levels for justifications and decision points for compliance.</summary>
<parameter name="document_type">guidance

title: FAR Companion Part 10 Market Research Guidance (Version 2.0)
summary: Comprehensive guidance on conducting market research as part of the federal acquisition process, outlining a five-step incremental approach from understanding the marketplace through exploring alternatives. Provides detailed information on data sources, industry engagement methods, and documentation requirements for market research activities prior to formal solicitation.

title: FAR Companion - Parts 11, 35, 37, 39 Requirements Development (Version 2.0)
summary: Comprehensive guidance on requirements development and acquisition strategies across FAR Parts 11, 35, 37, and 39, covering modular acquisition, rapid capabilities integration, outcome-based contracting, R&D contracting approaches, service contracting, and ICT acquisitions. Emphasizes collaborative requirements development, performance-based specifications, merit-based evaluation, and strategic planning for technology acquisitions aligned with agency objectives.

title: FAR Companion Part 15: Negotiation and Source Selection Guidance
summary: Comprehensive guidance document on FAR Part 15 negotiation and source selection procedures, covering deficiency definitions, early industry exchanges, best value continuum approaches (LPTA, Tradeoff, HTR-FRP), phased evaluation and down-select processes, oral presentations, amendments for alternate solutions, and rating systems. Provides practical examples and best practices for contracting officers and acquisition professionals to conduct fair and effective competitive procurements.

title: FAR Companion - Part 16 Contract Types Guidance
summary: Comprehensive guidance on Federal Acquisition Regulation Part 16 contract types, covering selection criteria, firm-fixed-price contracts, cost-reimbursement contracts, incentive structures, and streamlined ordering procedures for indefinite-quantity and multiple-award contracts.

title: FAR Companion Part 17: Special Contracting Methods
summary: Comprehensive guidance on special contracting methods under FAR Part 17, covering multiyear contracting, options, continuity clauses, leader company contracting, and reverse auctions. Provides practical considerations for structuring contracts, determining fair and reasonable pricing, and ensuring compliance with competition requirements while achieving cost savings and operational benefits.

title: FAR Companion - Part 19 Small Business Programs
summary: Comprehensive guidance on FAR Part 19 small business programs covering innovative procurement approaches, set-asides, HUBZone preferences, subcontracting plans, and contracting officer strategies to reduce bid burdens and encourage small business participation in federal acquisitions.

title: FAR Companion - Part 22 Labor Laws and Wage Determinations
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal labor laws, wage determinations, and prevailing wage requirements for construction and service contracts. Covers Department of Labor regulations, wage determination procedures, applicability rules, and compliance requirements for federal contractors.

title: FAR Companion - Part 27 Patents, Data, and Copyrights
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal acquisition policy regarding patents, data rights, and copyrights under government contracts. Covers contractor patent rights, government licensing, royalty reporting, invention disclosure protection, data acquisition, and cosponsored R&D activities.

title: FAR Companion Part 32: Contract Financing - Payments, Advance Payments, and Limitation of Funds
summary: This FAR Companion document provides guidance on contract financing under FAR Part 32, covering payment due dates and procedures, applicability of advance payments in various contract scenarios, deferment of collection processes, and tailoring options for limitation of cost/funds clauses. It details the roles of contracting officers, payment offices, and billing offices in managing contract financing requests and addresses special circumstances such as financially weak contractors, classified contracts, and remote operations.

title: FAR Companion Part 33: Protests, Disputes, and Appeals - Guidance and Best Practices
summary: This FAR Companion document provides practical guidance on managing protests, disputes, and appeals in federal acquisitions. It covers solicitation clarity, technical evaluation safeguards, documentation practices, GAO bid protest procedures, debriefing best practices, and alternative dispute resolution strategies to minimize protest risk and strengthen the government's defensibility of acquisition decisions.

title: FAR Companion Part 36: Construction and Architect-Engineer Contracts
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal acquisition of construction services, comparing Design-Bid-Build and Design-Build methodologies. Covers presolicitation procedures, evaluation criteria emphasizing qualifications, site visits, government estimates, and collaborative contract administration for design-build projects.

title: FAR Companion Part 40: Information Security and Supply Chain Risk Management in Federal Acquisitions
summary: This guidance document provides comprehensive direction for integrating security and supply chain risk management throughout the federal acquisition lifecycle. It establishes cross-functional team composition requirements, outlines security controls at key decision points from market research through contract closeout, and defines supply chain risk evaluation criteria including foreign control, functionality, source capability, and contractor mitigation capacity. The document emphasizes enhanced security outcomes for higher-risk procurements involving sensitive information and critical technologies.

title: FAR Companion Part 42: Contract Administration Procedures and Guidance
summary: Comprehensive guidance on contract administration functions including interagency agreements, cognizant federal agency responsibilities, contract correspondence routing, contractor facility visits, postaward orientation procedures, and performance monitoring. Covers contract administration office (CAO) coordination, documentation requirements, and best practices for ensuring successful contract performance and risk mitigation.

title: FAR Companion Part 44: Subcontracting Policies and Contractor Purchasing System Reviews
summary: This guidance document provides comprehensive policies on subcontracting requirements, consent and notification procedures, and contractor purchasing system reviews (CPSR). It outlines the Contracting Officer's evaluation considerations for subcontract approval, requirements for commercial contracts, and procedures for reviewing and approving contractor purchasing systems to protect government interests.

title: FAR Companion Part 48: Value Engineering - Principles, VECP Sharing, and Best Practices
summary: This guidance document explains Value Engineering (VE) techniques and Value Engineering Change Proposals (VECPs) under FAR Part 48, covering both voluntary incentive and mandatory program requirement approaches. It provides best practices for evaluating VECPs objectively, establishing tailored sharing periods and rates, and ensuring contractors receive fair compensation for accepted proposals without downward adjustment of profit or fee.

title: FAR Companion Part 4: Contract Files and Administration
summary: This guidance document outlines the required contents and organization of contract files throughout the acquisition lifecycle, from presolicitation through closeout. It specifies documentation requirements for three primary file types: Contracting Office File, Contract Administration Office File, and Paying Office File, while also addressing centralized acquisitions reporting and low-risk contract closeout procedures.

title: FAR Companion Part 5 Publicizing Guidance - Version 2.0
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal acquisition publicizing requirements emphasizing plain language communication, active industry engagement, and modern digital platforms. Covers best practices for presolicitation notices, market research, response time considerations, and expanding reach beyond SAM.gov to attract qualified vendors and improve acquisition outcomes.

title: FAR Companion Part 6: Competition and JOFOC Guidance
summary: Comprehensive guidance on optimizing competition through early acquisition planning and preparing strong Justifications and Approvals (J&A) for FAR Part 6 exceptions. Emphasizes market research as the cornerstone of competitive analysis, proper alignment of legal authorities to actual circumstances, and using planning to reduce reliance on competition exceptions while demonstrating sound stewardship of taxpayer dollars.

title: FAR Companion - Part 7 Acquisition Planning (Complete Guidance)
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal acquisition planning under FAR Part 7, covering oral acquisition plans, early planning principles, industry engagement, team composition, and detailed content requirements for routine and complex procurements. Includes best practices for optimizing competition, innovation, and small business participation.

title: FAR Companion - Part 8 Required Sources and Category Management; Part 12 Commercial Acquisitions
summary: Comprehensive guidance on federal acquisition requirements under FAR Parts 8 and 12, covering mandatory source requirements (excess property, UNICOR, AbilityOne), category management hierarchy, commercial product/service acquisitions, simplified procedures, and protest management strategies.

title: FAR Companion - Part 9 Contractor Qualifications
summary: Guidance on FAR Part 9 contractor qualifications covering contractor team arrangements, defense production pools, cost considerations, special standards, preaward surveys, and qualified bidders lists. Provides contracting officers with procedures for assessing contractor responsibility and capability.

title: FY26 Streamlined Market Research Report Template
summary: This is a standardized template for conducting streamlined market research in support of federal acquisitions. It provides a structured framework for documenting market research methods, findings, and recommendations across various acquisition scenarios, including commercial products/services, consolidations, and bundled contracts. The template guides contracting officers through analysis of customary commercial terms, Buy American compliance, and small business considerations.

title: NIH File Reviewer's Checklist (FRC) - OALM Acquisition Compliance Review Standards
summary: Comprehensive checklist used by NIH's Office of Acquisition and Logistics Management (OALM) during Acquisition Compliance Reviews to assess acquisition file completeness and regulatory compliance. Covers general requirements, acquisition planning, solicitation, evaluation, award, modifications, receiving, and closeout phases with color-coded rating system (Green/Yellow/Red/NA).

title: GAO Decision B-302358: IDIQ Minimum Obligation Requirements and Recording Statute Compliance
summary: GAO decision establishing that IDIQ contracts require agencies to obligate the guaranteed minimum amount at the time of contract award, not when task orders are issued. The decision clarifies the distinction between IDIQ contracts and multiyear contracts under 41 U.S.C. § 254c, and holds that failure to timely record the minimum obligation violates the recording statute (31 U.S.C. § 1501(a)) and risks Antideficiency Act violations. Customs violated the recording statute by failing to obligate $25M minimum at contract award and instead obligating funds from the wrong fiscal year.

title: GAO Decision B-321640: IDIQ Guaranteed Minimum - Bona Fide Needs Rule Violation
summary: GAO decision establishing that IDIQ contract guaranteed minimums must reflect genuine bona fide needs of the agency at the time of contract award, not serve as a mechanism to "park" funds at fiscal year-end. SBA violated this rule by obligating $1.3M in FY 2009 and $1.9M in FY 2010 without issuing corresponding task orders until the following fiscal years, demonstrating no current-year need existed when obligations were recorded.

title: GAO Decision B-409528: Consolidation Analysis and Small Business Jobs Act Requirements - OS3 Office Supplies Procurement
summary: GAO decision upholding GSA's consolidation of 400+ office supply MAS vendors into 24 multiple-award IDIQ contracts worth $1.25 billion. Establishes that Small Business Jobs Act consolidation requirements do not mandate detailed quantified cost-benefit analysis, but require market research, identification of alternatives, written determination, identification of negative impacts, and documented steps to include small businesses. Provides practical guidance on consolidation analysis documentation standards and acceptable levels of analysis rigor.

title: GAO Decision B-409917.11: Gella LLC - Failure to Rebut Agency Arguments in Protest Comments
summary: GAO decision establishing that protesters must substantively rebut detailed agency responses to protest allegations or face procedural default and protest denial. The decision addresses key personnel evaluation standards requiring experience similar in scope to the contract type, related experience evaluation requiring size and scope similarity, and the requirement that reevaluations reasonably reflect proposal merits rather than maintain consistency with prior evaluations. Protesters filing generic disagreement statements without engaging specific agency arguments provide no basis for GAO to question agency reasonableness.

title: GAO Decision B-410074: Task Order Consolidation Analysis Requirements Under Small Business Jobs Act
summary: GAO decision establishing that consolidation analysis is required for task orders under multiple-award contracts (MACs) when consolidating two or more prior contracts exceeding $2M, and that agencies must conduct market research, assess small business impact, and obtain written SPE/CAO determination before issuing such solicitations. The decision also addresses cost reimbursement standards, finding the Air Force unduly delayed corrective action and awarding protest costs for the clearly meritorious consolidation challenge.

title: GAO Decision B-413091: Employee Compensation Plan Evaluation - Burdened vs. Unburdened Rates Analysis
summary: GAO sustained protest of Air Force IT support task order award, finding agency improperly evaluated Employee Compensation Plans by comparing burdened labor rates (including overhead, G&A, profit) instead of actual compensation and fringe benefits as required by FAR 52.222-46. The decision establishes critical guidance that ECP evaluations must analyze unburdened rates representing actual employee compensation, not total government costs, and requires adequate documentation of labor category mapping comparisons.

title: GAO Decision B-413104.32: Technical Approach vs. Experience Only - Trillion Technology Solutions, Inc.
summary: GAO decision denying protest where NIH reasonably evaluated offeror's proposal as unacceptable for failing to describe technical approach and methodology for future performance, despite providing cogent descriptions of past experience. Establishes critical distinction that describing what was accomplished on prior contracts is insufficient without explicit explanation of how work will be performed and what methodology will be used on the solicitation.

title: GAO Decision B-413104.35/38: Technical Approach vs. Experience in Proposal Evaluation
summary: GAO decision denying protest by BITS regarding NIH's rejection of its CIO-SP3 GWAC proposal for failing to provide a forward-looking technical approach and methodology, instead submitting only past experience descriptions. The decision establishes that while RFPs may encourage experience examples, they cannot substitute for required technical approach and methodology, and that generic template proposals providing methodology are acceptable even if not customized.

title: GAO Decision B-413104.36: Shivoy Inc. - Cost Accounting System Verification Requirements for CTA Members
summary: GAO decision denying protest by Shivoy Inc. regarding NIH CIO-SP3 GWAC evaluation. Establishes that financial audit reports on financial statements do not satisfy FAR 16.301-3(a)(1) requirements for cost accounting system verification; offerors and all CTA members must provide verification from DCAA, DCMA, federal audit agencies, or third-party CPAs explicitly stating the accounting system is adequate for determining costs applicable to cost-reimbursement contracts. Decision clarifies the critical distinction between financial statement audits and cost accounting system adequacy verifications required for government contracting.

title: GAO Decision B-413104.40: Opus Group, LLC - CTA Encrypted Document Passwords
summary: GAO decision denying protest by Opus Group, LLC regarding NIH's rejection of proposal for failure to provide decryption passwords for encrypted financial documents submitted by contractor team arrangement (CTA) members. Establishes that encrypted documents without passwords are tantamount to failure to provide required documents, agency has no obligation to request missing passwords, and offerors bear responsibility for timely submission of all required materials including proper delivery confirmation.

title: GAO Decision B-413104.4: NextGen Consulting - CTA Conflicting Team Lead Information
summary: GAO decision denying protest of NIH CIO-SP3 GWAC proposal rejection due to deficient Contractor Team Arrangement (CTA) documentation. The CTA agreement contained conflicting information about team lead designation (NextGen vs. KOMPSYS), failed to clearly distinguish between FAR 9.601(1) CTA members and FAR 9.601(2) subcontractors, and lacked specific duties and responsibilities for each team member, making agency evaluation impossible. Decision establishes that offerors bear responsibility for submitting well-written, clear CTA documents with unambiguous team lead designation and specific task assignments.

title: GAO Decision B-413729: Out-of-Scope Task Order Violated CICA - Threat Management Group v. Air Force
summary: GAO sustained a protest by Threat Management Group against an Air Force task order (TO 76) for EOD training services under an R3 IDIQ contract, finding the task order violated the Competition in Contracting Act by exceeding the underlying contract's scope. The agency failed to provide adequate documentation establishing what specific services were requested, and available evidence showed some training services (CBRN training, controlled area training, medical training) fell outside the contract's Performance Work Statement scope, which was limited to demolition and handling of explosives training.

title: GAO Decision B-414103: Verdi Consulting v. HUD - Inadequate Evaluation Documentation for Price and Past Performance
summary: GAO sustained protest of HUD's award of Financial Operations Services IDIQ contract to Falcon Capital Advisors, finding three critical deficiencies: (1) past performance evaluation improperly assessed future capability (HERMIT system access) rather than past performance and was inadequately documented; (2) price evaluation failed to evaluate option year pricing as required by FAR 52.217-5 and RFP terms, considering only base year costs; (3) best-value tradeoff was inadequately documented with only two sentences and failed to compare all competitive range offerors or consider total evaluated prices. Agency's corrective action reevaluating past performance and technical factors did not cure defects because no new source selection decision was issued and price evaluation remained flawed.

title: GAO Decision B-417500: Coast to Coast Computer Products - BPA Consolidation Analysis and Small Business Set-Aside Requirements
summary: GAO decision establishing that consolidation analysis under the Small Business Jobs Act applies to task orders issued under BPAs, not to BPA establishment itself. The decision clarifies that agencies are not required to set aside FSS BPA establishments for small businesses and affirms that apparent solicitation improprieties must be protested before the closing date.

title: GAO Decision B-418193.2: Unissant, Inc. - Corrective Action for Inadequate Evaluation Documentation
summary: GAO decision denying protest of agency corrective action (task order termination and procurement restart) where the contracting officer acknowledged making source selection based on conclusory technical findings without adequate supporting documentation. Establishes that agencies have broad discretion to take corrective action when procurement actions are flawed and inadequately documented, and that GAO will not force agencies to proceed with awards they believe violate procurement law and regulation.

title: GAO Decision B-419831: Alpine Companies, Inc. - Procurement Integrity Act Reporting Requirements and Interested Party Status
summary: GAO decision dismissing protests by Alpine Companies, Inc. regarding a $54.9M HUD loan servicing contract award to ISN. The decision establishes that Alpine lacked interested party status due to its own proposal's technical unacceptability, that agencies are not required to seek clarifications for material term exceptions, and that Alpine failed to comply with PIA reporting requirements by declining to provide specific information about alleged violations within the 14-day reporting window.

title: GAO Decision B-419956.258: EPDS Filing Timeliness and Protest Procedures
summary: GAO decision establishing strict enforcement of protest filing deadlines, holding that email submissions to protests@gao.gov do not constitute valid filings when EPDS is functioning normally and that a 10-minute late EPDS filing is untimely with no exceptions. The decision clarifies that alternative email filing is only permitted when EPDS experiences system-wide technical failures during business hours, and provides guidance on proper protest procedures and filing requirements.

title: GAO Decision B-421835: Vertex Aerospace Past Performance Evaluation - Improper Blending of CPARS Ratings
summary: GAO sustained a protest against the Army's evaluation of Vertex Aerospace's past performance, finding the agency improperly "blended" CPARS ratings by tallying them without qualitative assessment of contract relevancy, thereby giving equal weight to ratings on relevant versus somewhat relevant contracts. The decision establishes that agencies must conduct qualitative analysis distinguishing between relevant and somewhat relevant contracts, document how relevancy affects confidence ratings, and ensure factual accuracy in source selection decisions.

title: GAO Decision B-421917: FSS Evaluation Errors - Kauffman and Associates, Inc. v. CMS
summary: GAO sustained a protest on multiple grounds against CMS's award of an FSS training services contract to Octane over Kauffman and Associates. The decision addresses critical evaluation errors including latent ambiguity in solicitation requirements, unequal evaluation of quotations, unreasonable technical assessments, improper past performance evaluation, and FSS pricing violations where the awardee proposed rates exceeding GSA schedule rates.

title: GAO Decision B-422681: Anika Systems, Inc. - Disparate Evaluation and Price Realism Analysis
summary: GAO sustained a protest by Anika Systems regarding disparate evaluation of technical proposals in a USCIS data strategy support services task order competition. The decision addresses three sustained grounds of disparate treatment (word frequency-based evaluation, ambiguous actor identification, and missed strength findings), one denied ground on unstated innovation criteria, and upholds agency discretion not to conduct price realism analysis when awardee's price is only 7.2% below average. The decision provides critical guidance on evaluation consistency, clarity standards, and price realism thresholds for fixed-price task orders.

title: GAO Decision B-423197: MicroTechnologies LLC - ECP Evaluation, Apples-to-Oranges Comparison, and SPRS Price Risk Analysis
summary: GAO sustained protests regarding Air Force's evaluation of professional employee compensation plans (FAR 52.222-46), improper "apples-to-oranges" comparison methodology mixing direct rates with fringe benefits, and failure to conduct required SPRS price risk analysis per DFARS 252.204-7024. Decision establishes that agencies must document specific analysis of program continuity, quality, and staff availability when evaluating rates below incumbent levels, and must compare like compensation elements (direct-to-direct or total-to-total, not mixed).

title: GAO Decision B-423281: Owl International Inc. v. Navy - ECP Evaluation Failure and Indirect Rate Cap Analysis
summary: GAO decision sustaining a protest by Owl International Inc. against the Navy's award to PCCI for ESSM and oil spill response services. The decision holds that the Navy failed to evaluate professional employee compensation (ECP) under FAR 52.222-46 despite incorporating the provision and requiring necessary cost data, but denies challenges to indirect rate cap treatment and technical/past performance evaluations. The decision provides critical guidance on calculating direct labor rates from burdened rates and the circumstances under which RFP provisions can modify standard cost realism analysis requirements.

title: GAO Decision B-423341: SMS Data Products Group, Inc. - Employee Compensation Plan Evaluation, Enhanced Debriefing Timeliness, and Misleading Discussions
summary: GAO decision addressing three key issues in a DoD task order competition: (1) timeliness of protest filed within 10 days of debriefing delivery despite untimely enhanced debriefing questions; (2) reasonableness of evaluating awardee's professional employee compensation plan as realistic despite 10% lower total price than protester; and (3) whether discussions were misleading when agency identified protester's rates as unrealistic and offered choice to revise or justify, but protester chose to increase rates. Decision establishes incumbent rate reliability methodology using 4% threshold and confirms that offering offerors choice between revising rates or providing justification does not constitute misleading discussions.

title: GAO Decision B-423546: SPRS Past Performance Evaluation, Contract Administration vs. Protest Jurisdiction, and New Pleading Standards
summary: GAO decision dismissing and denying protest grounds in a DLA simplified acquisition for vehicle tire cross chains, establishing that disputes over existing contract usage and performance explanations are contract administration matters outside GAO jurisdiction. The decision adopts the new FY2025 NDAA pleading standard requiring protesters to provide credible allegations supported by evidence, not bare assertions, and affirms agency discretion to pay price premiums for superior past performance using SPRS/VPH systems.

title: GAO Decision B-423552: Past Performance Evaluation of Affiliates, Joint Ventures, and Best-Value Tradeoff Analysis
summary: GAO bid protest decision addressing improper evaluation of past performance from affiliate companies and joint ventures, crediting offerors with work they did not perform, and inadequate qualitative comparison in best-value tradeoff analysis. The decision establishes requirements for agencies to properly document affiliate relationships, analyze joint venture roles, and conduct qualitative comparisons beyond adjectival ratings when evaluating proposals.

title: GAO Bid Protests - Essential Guide for Contracting Officers
summary: Comprehensive reference guide on GAO bid protest procedures, authority, filing requirements, and timeliness rules under the Competition in Contracting Act. Covers who may file, what can be protested, decision timelines, and key takeaways for contracting officers managing protests.

title: Government Furnished Property (GFP) Form
summary: This form documents Government-furnished property made available to contractors for contract performance. It requires identification of property by name, part number, manufacturer, and NSN, along with acquisition costs and condition status. The contractor bears responsibility for all costs related to property availability, including transportation, installation, and rehabilitation.

title: GSA Order-Level Materials (OLMs) Guidance for EAGLE GSA Schedule Order Support
summary: Comprehensive guidance on Order-Level Materials (OLMs) procedures for adding non-Schedule items to GSA Schedule orders. Covers OLM requirements, limitations (33.33% cost cap), pricing determination, contractor responsibilities, and compliance requirements under GSA Schedules authority.

title: HCA and SPE Approval Thresholds and Review Instructions (Updated November 2025)
summary: This reference document provides current approval thresholds and review procedures for HHS acquisition strategies, acquisition plans, and justifications and approvals (J&As), incorporating FAC 2025-06 updates effective October 1, 2025. It establishes dollar-based approval levels ranging from Contracting Officer to HHS Senior Procurement Executive, includes EAGLE system integration guidance, and specifies submission requirements and timelines for federal acquisition reviews.

title: HHS Streamlined Acquisition Plan (AP) Template
summary: This template provides the standardized format for completing Acquisition Plans (APs) at the Department of Health and Human Services, as required by FAR 7.1 and 41 USC 253(a). It guides acquisition planners through all required sections including needs assessment, cost analysis, competition strategy, contract type selection, and risk management to ensure comprehensive planning for all acquisitions above the simplified acquisition threshold.
